summary: Sabidussi's conjecture — that every Eulerian graph of minimum degree $4$ admits a cycle decomposition compatible with any prescribed Eulerian tour (no two consecutive tour-edges in the same cycle) — remains open in full generality. Known pre-2013 partial results include the case of planar graphs (Fleischner) and $K_5$-minor-free graphs (Fan and Zhang, 2000); a weak version for $80$-edge-connected graphs was also established. No verifiable post-2013 resolution of the general conjecture was found.
search_queries: Sabidussi conjecture Eulerian graph decomposition cycles consecutive edges Eulerian tour
Eulerian graph cycle decomposition prescribed tour no consecutive edges conjecture
Kotzig Sabidussi eulerian tour cycle decomposition graph theory arXiv
Fleischner eulerian tour cycle decomposition "consecutive edges" conjecture graph
notes: All known partial results (Fleischner for planar graphs; Fan–Zhang 2000 for K₅-minor-free graphs; compatible decomposition for 80-edge-connected graphs) predate the 2013 OPG posting and thus are not listed in since_posted. A Google Scholar AI summary claimed a 2024 Graphs-and-Combinatorics paper ('Perfect Pseudo-Matchings in Cubic Graphs', Fleischner, Gh, Klocker, doi:10.1007/s00373-024-02844-y) concludes that Sabidussi's Compatibility Conjecture is false, but this claim could not be verified: Springer required authentication, no arXiv preprint was found, and a direct Google Scholar description of the same paper contained no mention of Sabidussi's conjecture — strongly suggesting the earlier summary was a hallucination. The AJC volume 58 (February 2014) PDF 'A cycle decomposition conjecture for Eulerian graphs' was also inaccessible (binary PDF, content unreadable). Status is therefore reported as open with medium confidence.
